questions.question: Define the term 'neighborhood operator' in the context of image processing. Provide an example of a neighborhood operator.
questions.hint: A neighborhood operator is a mathematical function that operates on a local region of an image. An example of a neighborhood operator is the 'Mean Shift' operator, which calculates the mean of neighboring pixel values.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a convolution operation and a correlation operation in image processing?
questions.hint: The main difference between a convolution operation and a correlation operation is that a convolution operation slides a kernel over the entire image, whereas a correlation operation slides the entire image over the kernel.

questions.question: Explain the concept of a Gaussian Pyramid and its application in image processing. Provide an example of how it can be used to reduce the noise in an image.
questions.hint: A Gaussian Pyramid is a hierarchical representation of an image where each level of the pyramid is obtained by applying a Gaussian filter to the previous level. The pyramid can be used to reduce noise in an image by repeatedly applying a low-pass filter to the image at each level of the pyramid.
